title: Tachanun: The Most Unpopular Prayer and Why We Need It
title_zh: Tachanun:最不受欢迎的祷告——为何我们仍需要它
title_he: תחנון: התפילה הכי פחות פופולרית – ולמה אנחנו צריכים אותה
description: Why Jews rest their heads on their arms during this confessional prayer, when it's skipped, and what it teaches about vulnerability before God.
description_zh: 为何犹太人在这个忏悔祷告中将头靠在手臂上,何时跳过,以及它教导我们关于在上帝面前的脆弱性。
description_he: למה יהודים מניחים את ראשם על זרועותיהם במהלך תפילת הוידוי הזו, מתי משמיטים אותה, ומה היא מלמדת על פגיעות לפני ה'.
author: Torah Light Editorial Team
cultural_parallels: Confucian filial piety parallels to humility
Chinese historical period mapping (Western Zhou Dynasty)
header_image_prompt: A serene Chinese ink wash landscape showing a solitary figure bowing deeply beside a weathered rock at the edge of a misty river, surrounded by weeping willow trees. The composition uses muted grays and soft blues with touches of sepia, creating a contemplative and humble atmosphere. Morning mist rises from the water, partially obscuring distant mountains. The figure's posture conveys vulnerability and earnest supplication. Traditional shan shui style with delicate brushwork emphasizing the theme of human smallness before the vastness of the divine.
